AbsolutePathBuf.description:
A path that is guaranteed to be absolute and normalized (though it is not
guaranteed to be canonicalized or exist on the filesystem).

IMPORTANT: When deserializing an `AbsolutePathBuf`, a base path must be set
using [AbsolutePathBufGuard::new]. If no base path is set, the
deserialization will fail unless the path being deserialized is already
absolute.

AccountRateLimitsUpdatedNotification.description:
Sparse rolling rate-limit update.

Clients should merge available values into the most recent `account/rateLimits/read` response
or refetch that snapshot. Nullable account metadata may be unavailable in a rolling update and
does not clear a previously observed value.
AccountRateLimitsUpdatedNotification.title: AccountRateLimitsUpdatedNotification

extends.description:
Parent profile identifier from the selected permissions profile's
`extends` setting, when present.

id.description:
Identifier from `default_permissions` or the implicit built-in default,
such as `:workspace` or a user-defined `[permissions.<id>]` profile.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to no limit.

nextCursor.description:
Opaque cursor to pass to the next call to continue after the last item.
if None, there are no more items to return.

authProfile.description: Auth profile selected for model requests in this active peer, if known.

authProfileKind.description: Whether `authProfile` is the default root profile or a named profile.

peerId.description:
Active peer routing key returned by activeSession/list. For local
Codewith sessions this matches thread_id; bridge peers may use a
transport-scoped id.

sessionId.description:
Live session instance id for the loaded thread. This is not a durable
mailbox or remote machine id.

threadId.description: Codewith thread that owns the active peer registration.

senderLabel.description: Claimed sender label for attribution only.

senderThreadId.description:
Claimed sender thread for attribution only. activeSession/send does not
treat this as authentication or authorization.

targetPeerId.description: Active peer routing key from activeSession/list.

targetThreadId.description:
Legacy local Codewith-thread target. target_peer_id is preferred for
peers returned by activeSession/list.

messageId.description:
App-server-generated id for this active delivery attempt. It is not a
durable mailbox id or read receipt.

senderThreadId.description: Echoes the claimed sender_thread_id when present and valid.

source.description:
Optional provenance for client-supplied context from a local app store.

This metadata is for clients and auditability. It does not change
model-visible content or trust: `kind` remains the authority for whether
`value` is injected as untrusted user context or application context.

id.description: Stable record id inside the source namespace.

label.description: Optional user-facing label for rendering/editing the context source.

namespace.description:
Stable source namespace, for example `open-projects`, `open-todos`,
`open-conversations`, or `open-mementos`.

recordType.description:
Optional source record kind, for example `project`, `task`,
`conversation`, or `memento`.

read.description: This will be removed in favor of `entries`.

write.description: This will be removed in favor of `entries`.

AppBranding.description: EXPERIMENTAL - app metadata returned by app-list APIs.

AppInfo.description: EXPERIMENTAL - app metadata returned by app-list APIs.

isEnabled.description:
Whether this app is enabled in config.toml.
Example:
```toml
[apps.bad_app]
enabled = false
```

AppListUpdatedNotification.description: EXPERIMENTAL - notification emitted when the app list changes.
AppListUpdatedNotification.title: AppListUpdatedNotification

AppSummary.description: EXPERIMENTAL - app metadata summary for plugin responses.

ApprovalsReviewer.description: Configures who approval requests are routed to for review. Examples include sandbox escapes, blocked network access, MCP approval prompts, and ARC escalations. Defaults to `user`. `auto_review` uses a carefully prompted subagent to gather relevant context and apply a risk-based decision framework before approving or denying the request. The legacy value `guardian_subagent` is accepted for compatibility.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

forceRefetch.description: When true, bypass app caches and fetch the latest data from sources.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to a reasonable server-side value.

threadId.description: Optional thread id used to evaluate app feature gating from that thread's config.

nextCursor.description:
Opaque cursor to pass to the next call to continue after the last item.
If None, there are no more items to return.

oneOf.description: OpenAI API key provided by the caller and stored by Codewith.
ChatGPT OAuth managed by Codewith (tokens persisted and refreshed by Codewith).

[UNSTABLE] FOR OPENAI INTERNAL USE ONLY - DO NOT USE.

ChatGPT auth tokens are supplied by an external host app and are only
stored in memory. Token refresh must be handled by the external host app.
Programmatic Codewith auth backed by a registered Agent Identity.
Programmatic Codex auth backed by a personal access token.
The source did not record auth profile selection.
The default root auth profile was selected explicitly or by runtime default.
A named auth profile was selected.

AutoCompactTokenLimitScope.description:
Selects which part of the active context is charged against
`model_auto_compact_token_limit`.

oneOf.description: Count the full active context against the limit.
Count sampled output and later growth after the carried window prefix.

AutoReviewDecisionSource.description: [UNSTABLE] Source that produced a terminal approval auto-review decision.

ClientRequest.description: Request from the client to the server.

oneOf.description: NEW APIs
oneOf.title: Thread/startRequest

oneOf.description: Append raw Responses API items to the thread history without starting a user turn.
oneOf.title: Thread/injectItemsRequest

oneOf.description: Execute a standalone command (argv vector) under the server's sandbox.
oneOf.title: Command/execRequest

oneOf.description: Write stdin bytes to a running `command/exec` session or close stdin.
oneOf.title: Command/exec/writeRequest

oneOf.description: Terminate a running `command/exec` session by client-supplied `processId`.
oneOf.title: Command/exec/terminateRequest

oneOf.description: Resize a running PTY-backed `command/exec` session by client-supplied `processId`.
oneOf.title: Command/exec/resizeRequest

ClientRequest.title: ClientRequest

CodexErrorInfo.description:
This translation layer make sure that we expose codex error code in camel case.

When an upstream HTTP status is available (for example, from the Responses API or a provider),
it is forwarded in `httpStatusCode` on the relevant `codexErrorInfo` variant.

oneOf.description: Failed to connect to the response SSE stream.
oneOf.title: ResponseStreamConnectionFailedCodexErrorInfo

oneOf.description: The response SSE stream disconnected in the middle of a turn before completion.
oneOf.title: ResponseStreamDisconnectedCodexErrorInfo

oneOf.description: Reached the retry limit for responses.
oneOf.title: ResponseTooManyFailedAttemptsCodexErrorInfo

oneOf.description:
Returned when `turn/start` or `turn/steer` is submitted while the current active turn
cannot accept same-turn steering, for example `/review` or manual `/compact`.
oneOf.title: ActiveTurnNotSteerableCodexErrorInfo

CollaborationMode.description: Collaboration mode for a Codewith session.

CollaborationModeMask.description: EXPERIMENTAL - collaboration mode preset metadata for clients.

CommandExecOutputDeltaNotification.description:
Base64-encoded output chunk emitted for a streaming `command/exec` request.

These notifications are connection-scoped. If the originating connection
closes, the server terminates the process.

capReached.description:
`true` on the final streamed chunk for a stream when `outputBytesCap`
truncated later output on that stream.

processId.description:
Client-supplied, connection-scoped `processId` from the original
`command/exec` request.

CommandExecOutputDeltaNotification.title: CommandExecOutputDeltaNotification

oneOf.description: stdout stream. PTY mode multiplexes terminal output here.
stderr stream.

CommandExecParams.description:
Run a standalone command (argv vector) in the server sandbox without
creating a thread or turn.

The final `command/exec` response is deferred until the process exits and is
sent only after all `command/exec/outputDelta` notifications for that
connection have been emitted.

command.description: Command argv vector. Empty arrays are rejected.

cwd.description: Optional working directory. Defaults to the server cwd.

disableOutputCap.description:
Disable stdout/stderr capture truncation for this request.

Cannot be combined with `outputBytesCap`.

disableTimeout.description:
Disable the timeout entirely for this request.

Cannot be combined with `timeoutMs`.

env.description:
Optional environment overrides merged into the server-computed
environment.

Matching names override inherited values. Set a key to `null` to unset
an inherited variable.

outputBytesCap.description:
Optional per-stream stdout/stderr capture cap in bytes.

When omitted, the server default applies. Cannot be combined with
`disableOutputCap`.

processId.description:
Optional client-supplied, connection-scoped process id.

Required for `tty`, `streamStdin`, `streamStdoutStderr`, and follow-up
`command/exec/write`, `command/exec/resize`, and
`command/exec/terminate` calls. When omitted, buffered execution gets an
internal id that is not exposed to the client.

sandboxPolicy.description:
Optional sandbox policy for this command.

Uses the same shape as thread/turn execution sandbox configuration and
defaults to the user's configured policy when omitted. Cannot be
combined with `permissionProfile`.

size.description:
Optional initial PTY size in character cells. Only valid when `tty` is
true.

streamStdin.description:
Allow follow-up `command/exec/write` requests to write stdin bytes.

Requires a client-supplied `processId`.

streamStdoutStderr.description:
Stream stdout/stderr via `command/exec/outputDelta` notifications.

Streamed bytes are not duplicated into the final response and require a
client-supplied `processId`.

timeoutMs.description:
Optional timeout in milliseconds.

When omitted, the server default applies. Cannot be combined with
`disableTimeout`.

tty.description:
Enable PTY mode.

This implies `streamStdin` and `streamStdoutStderr`.

CommandExecParams.title: CommandExecParams

processId.description:
Client-supplied, connection-scoped `processId` from the original
`command/exec` request.

size.description: New PTY size in character cells.

stderr.description:
Buffered stderr capture.

Empty when stderr was streamed via `command/exec/outputDelta`.

stdout.description:
Buffered stdout capture.

Empty when stdout was streamed via `command/exec/outputDelta`.

CommandExecTerminalSize.description: PTY size in character cells for `command/exec` PTY sessions.

processId.description:
Client-supplied, connection-scoped `processId` from the original
`command/exec` request.

CommandExecWriteParams.description:
Write stdin bytes to a running `command/exec` session, close stdin, or
both.

closeStdin.description: Close stdin after writing `deltaBase64`, if present.

deltaBase64.description: Optional base64-encoded stdin bytes to write.

processId.description:
Client-supplied, connection-scoped `processId` from the original
`command/exec` request.

CommandExecWriteParams.title: CommandExecWriteParams

oneOf.description: User approved the command.

User approved the command and future prompts in the same session-scoped
approval cache should run without prompting.

User approved the command, and wants to apply the proposed execpolicy amendment so future
matching commands can run without prompting.
oneOf.title: AcceptWithExecpolicyAmendmentCommandExecutionApprovalDecision

oneOf.description: User chose a persistent network policy rule (allow/deny) for this host.
oneOf.title: ApplyNetworkPolicyAmendmentCommandExecutionApprovalDecision

oneOf.description: User denied the command. The agent will continue the turn.
User denied the command. The turn will also be immediately interrupted.

approvals_reviewer.description:
[UNSTABLE] Optional default for where approval requests are routed for
review.

filePath.description: Path to the config file to write; defaults to the user's `config.toml` when omitted.

reloadUserConfig.description: When true, hot-reload the updated user config into all loaded threads after writing.

oneOf.description: Managed preferences layer delivered by MDM (macOS only).
oneOf.title: MdmConfigLayerSource

oneOf.description: Managed config layer from a file (usually `managed_config.toml`).

file.description:
This is the path to the system config.toml file, though it is not
guaranteed to exist.

oneOf.title: SystemConfigLayerSource

oneOf.description: Enterprise-managed config layer delivered by the cloud config bundle.

id.description: Stable identifier for the delivered layer.

name.description:
Admin-facing name for the delivered layer. This is surfaced in
diagnostics so users know which cloud layer needs administrator
attention.

oneOf.title: EnterpriseManagedConfigLayerSource

oneOf.description:
User config layer from $CODEWITH_HOME/config.toml. CODEX_HOME remains
a compatibility override for direct native binaries. This layer is
special in that it is expected to be:
- writable by the user
- generally outside the workspace directory

file.description:
This is the path to the user's config.toml file, though it is not
guaranteed to exist.

profile.description:
Name of the selected profile-v2 config layered on top of the base
user config, when this layer represents one.

oneOf.title: UserConfigLayerSource

oneOf.description:
Path to a project config folder within a project. Codewith prefers
`.codewith/`; `.codex/` remains a legacy fallback. There could be
multiple config folders between `cwd` and the project/repo root.
oneOf.title: ProjectConfigLayerSource

oneOf.description: Session-layer overrides supplied via `-c`/`--config`.
oneOf.title: SessionFlagsConfigLayerSource

oneOf.description:
`managed_config.toml` was designed to be a config that was loaded
as the last layer on top of everything else. This scheme did not quite
work out as intended, but we keep this variant as a "best effort" while
we phase out `managed_config.toml` in favor of `requirements.toml`.
oneOf.title: LegacyManagedConfigTomlFromFileConfigLayerSource

cwd.description:
Optional working directory to resolve project config layers. If specified,
return the effective config as seen from that directory (i.e., including any
project layers between `cwd` and the project/repo root).

requirements.description: Null if no requirements are configured (e.g. no requirements.toml/MDM entries).

filePath.description: Path to the config file to write; defaults to the user's `config.toml` when omitted.

details.description: Optional extra guidance or error details.

path.description: Optional path to the config file that triggered the warning.

range.description: Optional range for the error location inside the config file.

filePath.description: Canonical path to the config file that was written.

oneOf.description: A reset credit was consumed and the eligible rate-limit windows were reset.
No current rate-limit window is eligible for a reset.
The account has no earned reset credits available.
The same idempotency key already completed a reset successfully.
The authenticated account no longer matches the account that selected the credit.
The backend returned an unrecognized reset outcome.

authProfile.description:
Auth profile used for this reset. `null` selects the default root auth;
omission leaves the app-server's selected auth profile unchanged.

creditId.description:
Opaque reset-credit identifier to redeem. When omitted, the backend selects the next
available credit.

expectedAccountIdentityFingerprint.description: Required opaque account fingerprint from the read that selected this reset credit.

idempotencyKey.description:
Identifies one logical reset attempt. A UUID is recommended; reuse the same value when
retrying that attempt.

accountIdentityFingerprint.description: Opaque equality-only fingerprint of the account resolved for this request.

details.description: Optional extra guidance, such as migration steps or rationale.

summary.description: Concise summary of what is deprecated.

announcement.description:
Announcement copy shown to users when the feature is introduced.
Null when this feature is not in beta.

defaultEnabled.description: Whether this feature is enabled by default.

description.description:
Short summary describing what the feature does.
Null when this feature is not in beta.

displayName.description:
User-facing display name shown in the experimental features UI.
Null when this feature is not in beta.

enabled.description: Whether this feature is currently enabled in the loaded config.

name.description: Stable key used in config.toml and CLI flag toggles.

stage.description: Lifecycle stage of this feature flag.

enablement.description:
Process-wide runtime feature enablement keyed by canonical feature name.

Only named features are updated. Omitted features are left unchanged.
Send an empty map for a no-op.
Feature enablement entries updated by this request.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to a reasonable server-side value.

threadId.description:
Optional loaded thread id. Pass this when showing feature state for an
existing thread so enablement is computed from that thread's refreshed
config, including project-local config for the thread's cwd.

nextCursor.description:
Opaque cursor to pass to the next call to continue after the last item.
If None, there are no more items to return.

oneOf.description: Feature is available for user testing and feedback.
Feature is still being built and not ready for broad use.
Feature is production-ready.
Feature is deprecated and should be avoided.
Feature flag is retained only for backwards compatibility.

cwds.description: Zero or more working directories to include for repo-scoped detection.

includeHome.description: If true, include detection under the user's home (~/.claude, ~/.codewith, etc.).

cwd.description: Null or empty means home-scoped migration; non-empty means repo-scoped migration.

oneOf.description: User approved the file changes.
User approved the file changes and future changes to the same files should run without prompting.
User denied the file changes. The agent will continue the turn.
User denied the file changes. The turn will also be immediately interrupted.

ForcedChatgptWorkspaceIds.description: Backward-compatible API shape for ChatGPT workspace login restrictions.

FsChangedNotification.description: Filesystem watch notification emitted for `fs/watch` subscribers.

changedPaths.description: File or directory paths associated with this event.

FsChangedNotification.title: FsChangedNotification

FsCopyParams.description: Copy a file or directory tree on the host filesystem.

recursive.description: Required for directory copies; ignored for file copies.

FsCopyParams.title: FsCopyParams

FsCreateDirectoryParams.description: Create a directory on the host filesystem.

recursive.description: Whether parent directories should also be created. Defaults to `true`.

FsCreateDirectoryParams.title: FsCreateDirectoryParams

FsGetMetadataParams.description: Request metadata for an absolute path.
FsGetMetadataParams.title: FsGetMetadataParams

createdAtMs.description: File creation time in Unix milliseconds when available, otherwise `0`.

isDirectory.description: Whether the path resolves to a directory.

isFile.description: Whether the path resolves to a regular file.

isSymlink.description: Whether the path itself is a symbolic link.

modifiedAtMs.description: File modification time in Unix milliseconds when available, otherwise `0`.

fileName.description: Direct child entry name only, not an absolute or relative path.

isDirectory.description: Whether this entry resolves to a directory.

isFile.description: Whether this entry resolves to a regular file.

FsReadDirectoryParams.description: List direct child names for a directory.
FsReadDirectoryParams.title: FsReadDirectoryParams

entries.description: Direct child entries in the requested directory.

FsReadFileParams.description: Read a file from the host filesystem.
FsReadFileParams.title: FsReadFileParams

FsRemoveParams.description:
Remove a file or directory tree from the host filesystem.

The target must resolve inside the session's authorized workspace scope; the
server rejects paths (including symlink escapes) outside every workspace root.

force.description:
Whether missing paths should be ignored. Defaults to `false`; ignoring
missing targets must be requested explicitly.

path.description: Absolute path to remove. Must be inside an authorized workspace root.

recursive.description:
Whether directory removal should recurse. Defaults to `false`; recursive
tree deletion must be requested explicitly.

FsRemoveParams.title: FsRemoveParams

FsUnwatchParams.description: Stop filesystem watch notifications for a prior `fs/watch`.
FsUnwatchParams.title: FsUnwatchParams

FsWatchParams.description: Start filesystem watch notifications for an absolute path.

path.description: Absolute file or directory path to watch.

watchId.description: Connection-scoped watch identifier used for `fs/unwatch` and `fs/changed`.

FsWatchParams.title: FsWatchParams

path.description: Canonicalized path associated with the watch.

FsWriteFileParams.description: Write a file on the host filesystem.
FsWriteFileParams.title: FsWriteFileParams

FunctionCallOutputContentItem.description:
Responses API compatible content items that can be returned by a tool call.
This is a subset of ContentItem with the types we support as function call outputs.

refreshToken.description:
When `true`, requests a proactive token refresh before returning.

In managed auth mode this triggers the normal refresh-token flow. In
external auth mode this flag is ignored. Clients should refresh tokens
themselves and call `account/login/start` with `chatgptAuthTokens`.

authProfile.description:
Auth profile used for this read. `null` selects the default root auth;
omission leaves the app-server's selected auth profile unchanged.

includeResetCreditDetails.description: Include detailed reset-credit rows even when no credits remain available.

accountIdentityFingerprint.description:
Process-scoped opaque fingerprint of the authenticated account.

`null` means the current ChatGPT credentials do not expose an account identifier. Clients
must not display or log this value and must treat its representation as opaque.

rateLimits.description: Backward-compatible single-bucket view; mirrors the historical payload.

rateLimitsByLimitId.description: Multi-bucket view keyed by metered `limit_id` (for example, `codex`).

GuardianApprovalReview.description:
[UNSTABLE] Temporary approval auto-review payload used by
`item/autoApprovalReview/*` notifications. This shape is expected to change
soon.

GuardianApprovalReviewStatus.description: [UNSTABLE] Lifecycle state for an approval auto-review.

GuardianRiskLevel.description: [UNSTABLE] Risk level assigned by approval auto-review.

GuardianUserAuthorization.description: [UNSTABLE] Authorization level assigned by approval auto-review.

message.description: Concise guardian warning message for the user.

threadId.description: Thread target for the guardian warning.

cwds.description: When empty, defaults to the current session working directory.

experimentalApi.description: Opt into receiving experimental API methods and fields.

optOutNotificationMethods.description:
Exact notification method names that should be suppressed for this
connection (for example `thread/started`).

InputModality.description: Canonical user-input modality tags advertised by a model.

oneOf.description: Plain text turns and tool payloads.
Image attachments included in user turns.

completedAtMs.description: Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) when this item lifecycle completed.

ItemGuardianApprovalReviewCompletedNotif.description:
[UNSTABLE] Temporary notification payload for approval auto-review. This
shape is expected to change soon.

completedAtMs.description: Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) when this review completed.

startedAtMs.description: Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) when this review started.

targetItemId.description:
Identifier for the reviewed item or tool call when one exists.

In most cases, one review maps to one target item. The exceptions are
- execve reviews, where a single command may contain multiple execve
  calls to review (only possible when using the shell_zsh_fork feature)
- network policy reviews, where there is no target item

A network call is triggered by a CommandExecution item, so having a
target_item_id set to the CommandExecution item would be misleading
because the review is about the network call, not the command execution.
Therefore, target_item_id is set to None for network policy reviews.

ItemGuardianApprovalReviewCompletedNotif.title: ItemGuardianApprovalReviewCompletedNotification

ItemGuardianApprovalReviewStartedNotific.description:
[UNSTABLE] Temporary notification payload for approval auto-review. This
shape is expected to change soon.

startedAtMs.description: Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) when this review started.

targetItemId.description:
Identifier for the reviewed item or tool call when one exists.

In most cases, one review maps to one target item. The exceptions are
- execve reviews, where a single command may contain multiple execve
  calls to review (only possible when using the shell_zsh_fork feature)
- network policy reviews, where there is no target item

A network call is triggered by a CommandExecution item, so having a
target_item_id set to the CommandExecution item would be misleading
because the review is about the network call, not the command execution.
Therefore, target_item_id is set to None for network policy reviews.

ItemGuardianApprovalReviewStartedNotific.title: ItemGuardianApprovalReviewStartedNotification

startedAtMs.description: Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) when this item lifecycle started.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

detail.description:
Controls how much MCP inventory data to fetch for each server.
Defaults to `Full` when omitted.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to a server-defined value.

nextCursor.description:
Opaque cursor to pass to the next call to continue after the last item.
If None, there are no more items to return.

accountLabel.description: Redacted account label for the selected auth profile, when available.

activeFlags.description:
Active-status flags from the thread runtime. Empty for non-active
sessions.

authProfile.description: Auth profile selected for model requests in this session, if known.

authProfileKind.description: Whether `authProfile` is unknown, the default root profile, or a named profile.

peer.description:
Live active-session routing metadata when this record can receive
in-memory activeSession/send traffic.

runtimeSessionId.description:
Live session instance id when known. Persisted-only records return null
because a durable thread id is not the same as a runtime session id.

threadId.description: Durable Codewith thread id for the session record.

oneOf.description:
[UNSTABLE] FOR OPENAI INTERNAL USE ONLY - DO NOT USE.
The access token must contain the same scopes that Codewith-managed ChatGPT auth tokens have.

accessToken.description:
Access token (JWT) supplied by the client.
This token is used for backend API requests and email extraction.

chatgptAccountId.description: Workspace/account identifier supplied by the client.

chatgptPlanType.description:
Optional plan type supplied by the client.

When `null`, Codewith attempts to derive the plan type from access-token
claims. If unavailable, the plan defaults to `unknown`.

oneOf.title: ChatgptAuthTokensv2::LoginAccountParams

authUrl.description: URL the client should open in a browser to initiate the OAuth flow.

userCode.description: One-time code the user must enter after signing in.

verificationUrl.description: URL the client should open in a browser to complete device code authorization.

McpServerInfo.description: Presentation metadata advertised by an initialized MCP server.

MessagePhase.description:
Classifies an assistant message as interim commentary or final answer text.

Providers do not emit this consistently, so callers must treat `None` as
"phase unknown" and keep compatibility behavior for legacy models.

oneOf.description:
Mid-turn assistant text (for example preamble/progress narration).

Additional tool calls or assistant output may follow before turn
completion.
The assistant's terminal answer text for the current turn.

ModeKind.description: Initial collaboration mode to use when the TUI starts.

defaultServiceTier.description: Catalog default service tier id for this model, when one is configured.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

includeHidden.description: When true, include models that are hidden from the default picker list.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to a reasonable server-side value.

modelGateway.description: Gateway id to scope model browsing. Omission preserves active/provider behavior.

modelProvider.description: Provider id whose models should be listed. Omission uses the active provider.

upstreamProvider.description: Aggregator upstream provider id, such as the prefix in OpenRouter model slugs.

nextCursor.description:
Opaque cursor to pass to the next call to continue after the last item.
If None, there are no more items to return.

modelGateway.description: Gateway id to scope provider browsing. Omission preserves the provider-only catalog.

managedAllowedDomainsOnly.description:
When true, only managed allowlist entries are respected while managed
network enforcement is active.

unixSockets.description: Canonical unix socket permission map for `experimental_network`.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

cwd.description: Optional working directory to resolve project config layers.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to the full result set.

nextCursor.description:
Opaque cursor to pass to the next call to continue after the last item.
If None, there are no more items to return.

description.description: Optional user-facing description for display in clients.

PlanDeltaNotification.description:
EXPERIMENTAL - proposed plan streaming deltas for plan items. Clients should
not assume concatenated deltas match the completed plan item content.
PlanDeltaNotification.title: PlanDeltaNotification

oneOf.description:
Plugin-service currently sends `"ENABLED"` for available remote plugins.
Codewith app-server exposes `"AVAILABLE"` in its API; the alias keeps
decoding compatible with that upstream response.

cwds.description: Optional working directories used to discover repo marketplaces.

installSuggestionPluginNames.description:
Additional uninstalled plugin names that should be returned when present locally.
This is used by mention surfaces that intentionally expose install entrypoints.

composerIcon.description: Local composer icon path, resolved from the installed plugin package.

composerIconUrl.description: Remote composer icon URL from the plugin catalog.

defaultPrompt.description:
Starter prompts for the plugin. Capped at 3 entries with a maximum of
128 characters per entry.

logo.description: Local logo path, resolved from the installed plugin package.

logoUrl.description: Remote logo URL from the plugin catalog.

screenshotUrls.description: Remote screenshot URLs from the plugin catalog.

screenshots.description: Local screenshot paths, resolved from the installed plugin package.

cwds.description:
Optional working directories used to discover repo marketplaces. When omitted,
only home-scoped marketplaces and the official curated marketplace are considered.

marketplaceKinds.description:
Optional marketplace kind filter. When omitted, only local marketplaces are queried, plus
the default remote catalog when enabled by feature flag.

path.description:
Local marketplace file path when the marketplace is backed by a local file.
Remote-only catalog marketplaces do not have a local path.

remoteVersion.description: Version of the remote shared plugin release when available.

oneOf.description:
The plugin is available in the remote catalog. Download metadata is
kept server-side and is not exposed through the app-server API.

stderr.description:
Buffered stderr capture.

Empty when stderr was streamed via `process/outputDelta`.

stderrCapReached.description:
Whether stderr reached `outputBytesCap`.

In streaming mode, stderr is empty and cap state is also reported on the
final stderr `process/outputDelta` notification.

stdout.description:
Buffered stdout capture.

Empty when stdout was streamed via `process/outputDelta`.

stdoutCapReached.description:
Whether stdout reached `outputBytesCap`.

In streaming mode, stdout is empty and cap state is also reported on the
final stdout `process/outputDelta` notification.

ProcessOutputDeltaNotification.description: Base64-encoded output chunk emitted for a streaming `process/spawn` request.

capReached.description:
True on the final streamed chunk for this stream when output was
truncated by `outputBytesCap`.

stream.description: Output stream this chunk belongs to.

oneOf.description: stdout stream. PTY mode multiplexes terminal output here.
stderr stream.

credits.description:

`null` means only `availableCount` is known.

ReasoningSummary.description:
A summary of the reasoning performed by the model. This can be useful for
debugging and understanding the model's reasoning process.
See https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/reasoning?api-mode=responses#reasoning-summaries

detached on a new thread (returned in `reviewThreadId`).

reviewThreadId.description:
Identifies the thread where the review runs.

For inline reviews, this is the original thread id.
For detached reviews, this is the id of the new review thread.

oneOf.description:
Use the current shared checkout and disallow session-managed worktree
create/attach operations for this thread.
Allow this session to manually create or attach managed worktrees.

Pull-request mode. The session must work inside an attached isolated
managed worktree before model turns can continue.

shortDescription.description: Legacy short_description from SKILL.md. Prefer SKILL.json interface.short_description.

SkillsChangedNotification.description:
Notification emitted when watched local skill files change.

Treat this as an invalidation signal and re-run `skills/list` with the
client's current parameters when refreshed skill metadata is needed.

cwds.description: When empty, defaults to the current session working directory.

forceReload.description: When true, bypass the skills cache and re-scan skills from disk.

byteRange.description: Byte range in the parent `text` buffer that this element occupies.

placeholder.description: Optional human-readable placeholder for the element, displayed in the UI.

agentNickname.description: Optional random unique nickname assigned to an AgentControl-spawned sub-agent.

agentRole.description: Optional role (agent_role) assigned to an AgentControl-spawned sub-agent.

authProfile.description: Auth profile selected for model requests in this thread, if known.

authProfileKind.description: Whether `authProfile` is unknown, the default root profile, or a named profile.

cliVersion.description: Version of the CLI that created the thread.

createdAt.description: Unix timestamp (in seconds) when the thread was created.

cwd.description: Working directory captured for the thread.

ephemeral.description: Whether the thread is ephemeral and should not be materialized on disk.

forkedFromId.description: Source thread id when this thread was created by forking another thread.

gitInfo.description: Optional Git metadata captured when the thread was created.

modelProvider.description: Model provider used for this thread (for example, 'openai').

parentThreadId.description: The ID of the parent thread. This will only be set if this thread is a subagent.

path.description: [UNSTABLE] Path to the thread on disk.

preview.description: Usually the first user message in the thread, if available.

sessionId.description: Session id shared by threads that belong to the same session tree.

source.description: Origin of the thread (CLI, VSCode, codewith exec, codewith app-server, etc.).

status.description: Current runtime status for the thread.

threadSource.description: Optional analytics source classification for this thread.

turns.description:
Only populated on `thread/resume`, `thread/rollback`, `thread/fork`, and `thread/read`
(when `includeTurns` is true) responses.
For all other responses and notifications returning a Thread,
the turns field will be an empty list.

updatedAt.description: Unix timestamp (in seconds) when the thread was last updated.

oneOf.description:
Audit notification emitted once a previously-surfaced permission request
reaches a terminal decision, whether answered by the client, denied by
default on timeout, or otherwise resolved by the server.
oneOf.title: PermissionResolvedThreadExternalAgentEvent

ThreadExternalAgentPermissionResolution.description:
How a surfaced external-agent permission request was ultimately resolved.

Included in [`ThreadExternalAgentEvent::PermissionResolved`] so clients can
keep an audit trail that distinguishes an explicit client decision from a
server-applied default-deny.

oneOf.description: The client answered the request via `thread/externalAgent/permission/respond`.
No response arrived before the server-side timeout elapsed; denied by default.

The request could not be parked for a response (e.g. the run is gone) and
was denied by default.

The run was cancelled, or a newer request replaced this one, before a
decision was made; denied by default.

ThreadForkParams.description:
There are two ways to fork a thread:
1. By thread_id: load the thread from disk by thread_id and fork it into a new thread.
2. By path: load the thread from disk by path and fork it into a new thread.

If using a non-empty path, the thread_id param will be ignored.
Empty string path values are treated as absent.

Prefer using thread_id whenever possible.

aggregatedOutput.description: The command's output, aggregated from stdout and stderr.

commandActions.description:
A best-effort parsing of the command to understand the action(s) it will perform.
This returns a list of CommandAction objects because a single shell command may
be composed of many commands piped together.

durationMs.description: The duration of the command execution in milliseconds.

processId.description: Identifier for the underlying PTY process (when available).

durationMs.description: The duration of the MCP tool call in milliseconds.
The duration of the dynamic tool call in milliseconds.

agentsStates.description: Last known status of the target agents, when available.

id.description: Unique identifier for this collab tool call.

model.description: Model requested for the spawned agent, when applicable.

prompt.description: Prompt text sent as part of the collab tool call, when available.

reasoningEffort.description: Reasoning effort requested for the spawned agent, when applicable.

receiverThreadIds.description:
Thread ID of the receiving agent, when applicable. In case of spawn operation,
this corresponds to the newly spawned agent.

senderThreadId.description: Thread ID of the agent issuing the collab request.

status.description: Current status of the collab tool call.

tool.description: Name of the collab tool that was invoked.

archived.description:
Optional archived filter; when set to true, only archived threads are returned.
If false or null, only non-archived threads are returned.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

cwd.description:
Optional cwd filter or filters; when set, only threads whose session cwd
exactly matches one of these paths are returned.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to a reasonable server-side value.

modelProviders.description:
Optional provider filter; when set, only sessions recorded under these
providers are returned. When present but empty, includes all providers.

searchTerm.description: Optional substring filter for the extracted thread title.

sortDirection.description: Optional sort direction; defaults to descending (newest first).

sortKey.description: Optional sort key; defaults to created_at.

sourceKinds.description:
Optional source filter; when set, only sessions from these source kinds
are returned. When omitted or empty, defaults to interactive sources.

useStateDbOnly.description:
If true, return from the state DB without scanning JSONL rollouts to
repair thread metadata. Omitted or false preserves scan-and-repair
behavior.

backwardsCursor.description:
Opaque cursor to pass as `cursor` when reversing `sortDirection`.
This is only populated when the page contains at least one thread.
Use it with the opposite `sortDirection`; for timestamp sorts it anchors
at the start of the page timestamp so same-second updates are not skipped.

nextCursor.description:
Opaque cursor to pass to the next call to continue after the last item.
if None, there are no more items to return.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to no limit.

data.description: Thread ids for sessions currently loaded in memory.

nextCursor.description:
Opaque cursor to pass to the next call to continue after the last item.
if None, there are no more items to return.

branch.description:
Omit to leave the stored branch unchanged, set to `null` to clear it,
or provide a non-empty string to replace it.

originUrl.description:
Omit to leave the stored origin URL unchanged, set to `null` to clear it,
or provide a non-empty string to replace it.

sha.description:
Omit to leave the stored commit unchanged, set to `null` to clear it,
or provide a non-empty string to replace it.

gitInfo.description:
Patch the stored Git metadata for this thread.
Omit a field to leave it unchanged, set it to `null` to clear it, or
provide a string to replace the stored value.

cwd.description:
Optional working directory relative to the thread cwd. Parent directory
traversal and absolute paths are rejected.

outputFile.description:
Optional output file relative to the monitor cwd. Parent directory
traversal, absolute paths, and symlink targets are rejected.

position.description: 1-based position in the pending agent-message mailbox.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to no limit.

includeTurns.description: When true, include turns and their items from rollout history.

itemsView.description: How much item detail to include for each returned turn; defaults to summary.

sortDirection.description: Optional turn pagination direction; defaults to descending.

ThreadResumeParams.description:
There are three ways to resume a thread:
1. By thread_id: load the thread from disk by thread_id and resume it.
2. By history: instantiate the thread from memory and resume it.
3. By path: load the thread from disk by path and resume it.

For non-running threads, the precedence is: history > non-empty path > thread_id.
If using history or a non-empty path for a non-running thread, the thread_id
param will be ignored.

If thread_id identifies a running thread, app-server rejoins that thread and
treats a non-empty path as a consistency check against the active rollout path.
Empty string path values are treated as absent.

Prefer using thread_id whenever possible.

approvalsReviewer.description: Reviewer currently used for approval requests on this thread.

sandbox.description:
Legacy sandbox policy retained for compatibility. Experimental clients
should prefer `activePermissionProfile` for profile provenance.

numTurns.description:
The number of turns to drop from the end of the thread. Must be >= 1.

This only modifies the thread's history and does not revert local file changes
that have been made by the agent. Clients are responsible for reverting these changes.

thread.description:
The updated thread after applying the rollback, with `turns` populated.

The ThreadItems stored in each Turn are lossy since we explicitly do not
persist all agent interactions, such as command executions. This is the same
behavior as `thread/resume`.

command.description:
Shell command string evaluated by the thread's configured shell.
Unlike `command/exec`, this intentionally preserves shell syntax
such as pipes, redirects, and quoting. This runs unsandboxed with full
access rather than inheriting the thread sandbox policy.
